description: Service Catalog Frontend - Angular UI for managing services and categories with foundational HTTP setup, NgRx SignalStore state management, and full CRUD UI components

userStories.title: Fix ESLint prefix and add HttpClient provider
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the ESLint prefix fixed to 'chairly' and HttpClient configured so that the frontend can make API calls.
acceptanceCriteria: libs/chairly/eslint.config.mjs component selector prefix changed from 'lib' to 'chairly' (kebab-case, element)
libs/chairly/eslint.config.mjs directive selector prefix changed from 'lib' to 'chairly' (camelCase, attribute)
libs/shared/eslint.config.mjs component selector prefix changed from 'lib' to 'chairly' (kebab-case, element)
libs/shared/eslint.config.mjs directive selector prefix changed from 'lib' to 'chairly' (camelCase, attribute)
provideHttpClient() added to the providers array in apps/chairly/src/app/app.config.ts
import { provideHttpClient } from '@angular/common/http' is present in app.config.ts
npx nx affected -t lint --base=main passes
npx nx affected -t test --base=main passes
npx nx affected -t build --base=main passes
userStories.notes: The lib ESLint configs currently use 'lib' as the prefix but CLAUDE.md requires 'chairly'. Fix both libs. HttpClient is needed for all API services in later stories.

userStories.title: API proxy config and environment setup
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a dev proxy for /api calls and an API_BASE_URL injection token so that the frontend can communicate with the backend.
acceptanceCriteria: proxy.conf.json created in apps/chairly/ with '/api' target 'http://localhost:5205' and changeOrigin: true
apps/chairly/project.json serve target updated with proxyConfig pointing to apps/chairly/proxy.conf.json
API_BASE_URL InjectionToken<string> created in libs/shared/src/lib/util/ with a sensible default value of '/api'
The injection token is exported from libs/shared/src/index.ts via the shared barrel export
npx nx affected -t lint --base=main passes
npx nx affected -t test --base=main passes
npx nx affected -t build --base=main passes
userStories.notes: The backend runs on localhost:5205. The proxy rewrites /api/* to the backend. The injection token allows services to use inject(API_BASE_URL) for the base URL. Export through the shared lib barrel.

userStories.title: TypeScript interfaces for service catalog DTOs
userStories.description: As a developer, I need TypeScript interfaces matching the backend DTOs so that API responses are type-safe.
acceptanceCriteria: ServiceResponse interface in libs/chairly/src/lib/services/util/ with fields: id (string), name (string), description (string | null), duration (string — TimeSpan format HH:MM:SS), price (number), categoryId (string | null), categoryName (string | null), isActive (boolean), sortOrder (number), createdAtUtc (string), createdBy (string), updatedAtUtc (string | null), updatedBy (string | null)
CreateServiceRequest interface with fields: name (string), description (string | null), duration (string), price (number), categoryId (string | null), sortOrder (number)
UpdateServiceRequest interface with same fields as CreateServiceRequest
ServiceCategoryResponse interface with fields: id (string), name (string), sortOrder (number), createdAtUtc (string), createdBy (string)
CreateServiceCategoryRequest interface with fields: name (string), sortOrder (number)
UpdateServiceCategoryRequest interface with same fields as CreateServiceCategoryRequest
All interfaces exported from a barrel file in services/util/
npx nx affected -t lint --base=main passes
npx nx affected -t build --base=main passes
userStories.notes: GUIDs from the backend map to string in TypeScript. TimeSpan maps to string in format 'HH:MM:SS'. DateTimeOffset maps to string (ISO 8601). These interfaces must exactly match the backend response DTOs from ServiceResponse.cs and ServiceCategoryResponse.cs.

userStories.title: ServiceCategoryApiService with unit tests
userStories.description: As a developer, I need an HTTP service for category CRUD operations so that the store can call the backend.
acceptanceCriteria: ServiceCategoryApiService created in libs/chairly/src/lib/services/data-access/
Uses inject(HttpClient) and inject(API_BASE_URL) from shared util
getAll() method: GET /api/service-categories, returns Observable<ServiceCategoryResponse[]>
create(request: CreateServiceCategoryRequest) method: POST /api/service-categories, returns Observable<ServiceCategoryResponse>
update(id: string, request: UpdateServiceCategoryRequest) method: PUT /api/service-categories/{id}, returns Observable<ServiceCategoryResponse>
delete(id: string) method: DELETE /api/service-categories/{id}, returns Observable<void>
All methods have explicit return types
Unit tests using HttpClientTestingModule (or provideHttpClientTesting) verify each method makes the correct HTTP call with correct URL, method, and body
npx nx affected -t lint --base=main passes
npx nx affected -t test --base=main passes
npx nx affected -t build --base=main passes
userStories.notes: Use Angular's HttpClient. Base URL from the API_BASE_URL injection token. Test with HttpTestingController to verify requests. Service should be providedIn: 'root' or provided at the route level — choose based on existing patterns.

userStories.title: ServiceApiService with unit tests
userStories.description: As a developer, I need an HTTP service for service CRUD operations so that the store can call the backend.
acceptanceCriteria: ServiceApiService created in libs/chairly/src/lib/services/data-access/
Uses inject(HttpClient) and inject(API_BASE_URL) from shared util
getAll() method: GET /api/services, returns Observable<ServiceResponse[]>
getById(id: string) method: GET /api/services/{id}, returns Observable<ServiceResponse>
create(request: CreateServiceRequest) method: POST /api/services, returns Observable<ServiceResponse>
update(id: string, request: UpdateServiceRequest) method: PUT /api/services/{id}, returns Observable<ServiceResponse>
delete(id: string) method: DELETE /api/services/{id}, returns Observable<void>
toggleActive(id: string) method: PATCH /api/services/{id}/toggle-active, returns Observable<ServiceResponse>
All methods have explicit return types
Unit tests verify each method makes the correct HTTP call
npx nx affected -t lint --base=main passes
npx nx affected -t test --base=main passes
npx nx affected -t build --base=main passes
userStories.notes: Same patterns as SCF-004. The toggleActive endpoint uses PATCH with no request body.

userStories.title: ServiceCategoryStore (NgRx SignalStore) with unit tests
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a SignalStore for categories so that UI components can reactively consume category state.
acceptanceCriteria: ServiceCategoryStore created in libs/chairly/src/lib/services/data-access/ using signalStore()
State shape: categories (ServiceCategoryResponse[]), isLoading (boolean), error (string | null)
loadCategories method: sets isLoading, calls API getAll(), updates categories on success, sets error on failure
createCategory method: calls API create(), adds result to categories array
updateCategory method: calls API update(), replaces the category in the array
deleteCategory method: calls API delete(), removes from array
Unit tests for all store methods using a mocked ServiceCategoryApiService
Tests verify state transitions (isLoading, categories updated, error set on failure)
npx nx affected -t lint --base=main passes
npx nx affected -t test --base=main passes
npx nx affected -t build --base=main passes
userStories.notes: Use @ngrx/signals signalStore with withState, withMethods, and withComputed as needed. Use rxMethod or patchState for async operations. The store will be provided at the route level in SCF-014.

userStories.title: ServiceStore (NgRx SignalStore) with unit tests
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a SignalStore for services so that UI components can reactively consume service state.
acceptanceCriteria: ServiceStore created in libs/chairly/src/lib/services/data-access/ using signalStore()
State shape: services (ServiceResponse[]), isLoading (boolean), error (string | null)
Computed signal: activeServices — filters services where isActive === true
Computed signal: servicesByCategory — groups services by categoryId (Record<string, ServiceResponse[]>) with a 'uncategorized' key for null categoryId
loadServices method: sets isLoading, calls API getAll(), updates services on success, sets error on failure
createService method: calls API create(), adds result to services array
updateService method: calls API update(), replaces the service in the array
deleteService method: calls API delete(), removes from array
toggleActive method: calls API toggleActive(), replaces the service in the array with the toggled version
Unit tests for all store methods and computed signals
npx nx affected -t lint --base=main passes
npx nx affected -t test --base=main passes
npx nx affected -t build --base=main passes
userStories.notes: Same patterns as SCF-006. The computed signals derive from the services array. servicesByCategory groups by categoryId, using 'uncategorized' as the key when categoryId is null.

userStories.title: Shared ConfirmationDialogComponent
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a reusable confirmation dialog component so that delete actions can prompt the user.
acceptanceCriteria: ConfirmationDialogComponent created in libs/shared/src/lib/ui/
Uses native HTML <dialog> element (no third-party modal library)
Signal inputs: title (string), message (string), confirmLabel (string, default 'Confirm'), cancelLabel (string, default 'Cancel'), isDestructive (boolean, default false)
OutputEmitterRef events: confirmed (void), cancelled (void)
Public open() and close() methods to control the dialog
Styled with Tailwind CSS classes — centered modal with overlay backdrop
When isDestructive is true, confirm button uses red/danger styling
OnPush change detection, standalone component
Component selector: chairly-confirmation-dialog
Exported from libs/shared/src/index.ts
Unit test verifies dialog opens, emits confirmed on confirm click, emits cancelled on cancel click
npx nx affected -t lint --base=main passes
npx nx affected -t test --base=main passes
npx nx affected -t build --base=main passes
userStories.notes: Use the native <dialog> element with showModal()/close() methods. Style the backdrop with ::backdrop pseudo-element via Tailwind. This is in shared/ui/ because it will be reused across domains.

userStories.title: DurationPipe and helper functions with unit tests
userStories.description: As a developer, I need duration conversion helpers so that .NET TimeSpan values can be displayed as human-readable minutes.
acceptanceCriteria: parseDuration(timeSpan: string): number function in libs/chairly/src/lib/services/util/ — converts 'HH:MM:SS' to total minutes (e.g. '01:30:00' → 90)
formatDurationToTimeSpan(minutes: number): string function — converts minutes to 'HH:MM:SS' (e.g. 90 → '01:30:00')
DurationPipe (name: 'duration') in libs/chairly/src/lib/services/util/ — uses parseDuration to transform TimeSpan string to 'X min' or 'Xh Ymin' display format
Unit tests for parseDuration with edge cases: '00:00:00' → 0, '00:45:00' → 45, '02:00:00' → 120
Unit tests for formatDurationToTimeSpan with edge cases: 0 → '00:00:00', 60 → '01:00:00'
Unit tests for DurationPipe transform output
npx nx affected -t lint --base=main passes
npx nx affected -t test --base=main passes
npx nx affected -t build --base=main passes
userStories.notes: The backend uses .NET TimeSpan serialized as 'HH:MM:SS'. The frontend needs to convert this to minutes for display and form inputs, and back to TimeSpan format for API requests. The pipe is for template display only.

userStories.title: CategoryPanelComponent (presentational)
userStories.description: As a user, I want a side panel to manage service categories with inline add, edit, and delete.
acceptanceCriteria: CategoryPanelComponent created in libs/chairly/src/lib/services/ui/
Signal inputs: categories (ServiceCategoryResponse[]), isLoading (boolean)
OutputEmitterRef events: categoryCreated (CreateServiceCategoryRequest), categoryUpdated ({id: string, request: UpdateServiceCategoryRequest}), categoryDeleted (string — the category id)
Displays a list of categories sorted by sortOrder
Each category row shows name and sort order with edit/delete icon buttons
Add button at the top opens an inline form (input field + save/cancel buttons) — no modal
Edit button on a row switches that row to inline edit mode (input field + save/cancel)
Delete button emits categoryDeleted event (parent handles confirmation)
Styled with Tailwind CSS — vertical panel layout suitable for a sidebar
OnPush change detection, standalone component, selector: chairly-category-panel
No function calls in templates — use signals or helper methods returning signals where needed
npx nx affected -t lint --base=main passes
npx nx affected -t test --base=main passes
npx nx affected -t build --base=main passes
userStories.notes: This is a presentational (dumb) component. It receives data via inputs and emits events. The parent smart component handles API calls. Inline editing means the list item transforms into an editable form in-place.

userStories.title: ServiceFormDialogComponent (presentational)
userStories.description: As a user, I want a modal form to create or edit a service with all required fields.
acceptanceCriteria: ServiceFormDialogComponent created in libs/chairly/src/lib/services/ui/
Uses native <dialog> element
Signal inputs: categories (ServiceCategoryResponse[]) for the category dropdown, service (ServiceResponse | null) — null for create mode, populated for edit mode
OutputEmitterRef events: saved (CreateServiceRequest | UpdateServiceRequest), cancelled (void)

Reactive form with typed FormGroup: name (required, maxLength 150), description (optional, maxLength 2000), duration (required, number input in minutes), price (required, number, min 0), categoryId (optional, select dropdown), sortOrder (number)
Duration input is in minutes — on save, converted to TimeSpan format using formatDurationToTimeSpan helper
When service input is set (edit mode), form is pre-populated with existing values (duration converted from TimeSpan to minutes using parseDuration)
Public open() and close() methods
Form validation messages displayed inline
Styled with Tailwind CSS — modal with form layout
OnPush change detection, standalone component, selector: chairly-service-form-dialog
npx nx affected -t lint --base=main passes
npx nx affected -t test --base=main passes
npx nx affected -t build --base=main passes
userStories.notes: This is a presentational component. The parent opens/closes it and handles the saved event. Duration conversion uses the helpers from SCF-009. The form should reset when opened for create mode.

userStories.title: ServiceTableComponent (presentational)
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see all services in a table with key information and action buttons.
acceptanceCriteria: ServiceTableComponent created in libs/chairly/src/lib/services/ui/
Signal inputs: services (ServiceResponse[]), isLoading (boolean)
OutputEmitterRef events: editClicked (ServiceResponse), deleteClicked (ServiceResponse), toggleActiveClicked (ServiceResponse)

Table columns: Name, Category (categoryName or '—' if null), Duration (using DurationPipe), Price (formatted as currency), Status (active/inactive badge), Actions (edit, toggle active, delete buttons)
Active status shown as green badge, inactive as gray badge
Loading state shows a simple loading indicator or skeleton
Empty state message when no services exist
Styled with Tailwind CSS — clean table with hover rows
OnPush change detection, standalone component, selector: chairly-service-table
Uses DurationPipe in the template for duration column
npx nx affected -t lint --base=main passes
npx nx affected -t test --base=main passes
npx nx affected -t build --base=main passes
userStories.notes: This is a presentational component. It displays data and emits events. The DurationPipe from SCF-009 is used in the template. Price can be formatted with Angular's CurrencyPipe.

userStories.title: ServiceListPageComponent (smart container)
userStories.description: As a user, I want a single page at /services that shows the service table and category panel, with modal dialogs for create/edit and confirmation for delete.
acceptanceCriteria: ServiceListPageComponent created in libs/chairly/src/lib/services/feature/
Injects ServiceStore and ServiceCategoryStore
On init, calls loadServices() and loadCategories() on both stores
Template layout: main content area with ServiceTableComponent, sidebar with CategoryPanelComponent
Add Service button in the header opens ServiceFormDialogComponent in create mode
Edit action from table opens ServiceFormDialogComponent in edit mode with the selected service
Delete action from table opens ConfirmationDialogComponent with isDestructive=true, on confirm calls store.deleteService()
Toggle active action from table calls store.toggleActive()
Category panel events wired to ServiceCategoryStore methods (create, update, delete with confirmation)
Uses viewChild() signal queries to reference dialog components
OnPush change detection, standalone component, selector: chairly-service-list-page
npx nx affected -t lint --base=main passes
npx nx affected -t test --base=main passes
npx nx affected -t build --base=main passes
userStories.notes: This is the smart container that orchestrates everything. It connects stores to presentational components. Use viewChild() (signal-based) to get references to dialog components for open/close. Layout should be responsive — sidebar collapses on small screens or stacks below.

userStories.title: Lazy-loaded /services route with store providers
userStories.description: As a user, I want to navigate to /services to see the service catalog management page.
acceptanceCriteria: services.routes.ts created in libs/chairly/src/lib/services/feature/ with route config
Route provides ServiceStore and ServiceCategoryStore at the route level (stores created on navigate, destroyed on leave)
Route also provides ServiceApiService and ServiceCategoryApiService if not providedIn root
apps/chairly/src/app/app.routes.ts updated with lazy-loaded route: { path: 'services', loadChildren: () => import(...).then(m => m.servicesRoutes) }
Default route ('') redirects to '/services'
Navigating to /services renders ServiceListPageComponent
npx nx affected -t lint --base=main passes
npx nx affected -t test --base=main passes
npx nx affected -t build --base=main passes
userStories.notes: Stores are provided at the route level so they are scoped to the /services route. Use Angular's route providers array. The lazy import path should use the @org/chairly-lib alias or a relative path depending on what works with the Nx setup.

userStories.title: Final integration verification and cleanup
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to verify all lint, test, and build checks pass and fix any remaining issues.
acceptanceCriteria: npx nx affected -t lint --base=main passes with zero warnings
npx nx affected -t test --base=main passes with all tests green
npx nx affected -t build --base=main passes with zero errors
All components use OnPush change detection
All components are standalone (no NgModules)
No 'any' types in any TypeScript file
No console.log statements in any file
All functions have explicit return types
All signal-based APIs used (input(), model(), viewChild(), OutputEmitterRef) — no decorators
Component prefix is 'chairly-' on all components
No function calls in Angular templates — only signals, pipes, and property access
Sheriff module boundary rules are not violated (no cross-domain imports)
All barrel exports are properly set up
userStories.notes: This is a cleanup/verification story. Fix any remaining lint errors, failing tests, or convention violations. Ensure everything matches CLAUDE.md requirements. If everything already passes, just verify and mark as done.
